the diagram models a system where user information is accessed through a datamanager component.
the datamanager provides access to plant data (reading, inserting, modifying) and diary data (reading, inserting, modifying).
the diagram does not show any specific user interactions or error handling.

the diagram models the user authentication process and interaction with a gemini ai service.
the user authenticates with firebase authentication.
firebase authentication stores an image in personal storage and retrieves an image.
the gemini ai service receives text input from the user via the asktext component.

localuser -> authentication — message="request dati utente";
authentication -> localuser — message="new localuser";
main -> main — message="done";
main -> localuser: token = gettoken()

the diagram models the login process.
the user interface initiates the process by requesting a token.
the token is sent to the local user service, which then requests user data from the authentication service.
the authentication service creates a new localuser object and returns it to the local user service.
the main interface receives confirmation ("done").

the diagram models the process of adding a plant photo to a system.
the process involves sending the photo to a gemini ai api for species identification, storing the identified species in firebase storage, and then downloading the original photo with its url.
the diagram does not show error handling or alternative flows (e.g., if the ai api fails or the storage operation fails).

main -> firebase storage — type=message; label="request foto per ogni specie presente"; explanation="main requests photos for each species present"
firebase storage -> main — type=message; label="download uri di ogni foto richiesta"; explanation="firebase storage downloads the uris of requested photos"

firebase storage -> main — download uri di ogni foto richiesta
the diagram models a process where the main component requests access to personal storage from firebase storage.
it then requests photos for each species present, and firebase storage downloads the uris of those photos.
this appears to be a simple retrieval process, likely for displaying images associated with different species.
